data.title: Market Chatter: ByteDance Said to Hold Up Negotiations for TikTok US Sale as China Seen to Pull Out
data.description:
ByteDance is said to stall negotiations for the sale of TikTok's US operations as it awaits the green light from the Chinese government, The Washington Post reported Wednesday, citing three people familiar with the talks.

The development comes even as the allies of US President Donald Trump are scrambling to sell the app to a US bidder, the report said, citing the sources.

Beijing is likely to let TikTok's US operations fade away than approve the sale as it looks to wait for a "grand deal" that may include more favorable trade and tech approaches, the report said, citing sources.

The tactic could work against Trump's efforts to "save TikTok" by suspending the implementation of a law that would force the sale of the app to an American bidder or shut down US operations, The Washington Post said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Korea Zinc Swings to Loss in Q4 2024 Despite Higher Sales
data.description:
Korea Zinc swung to a net loss attributable to shareholders of the parent of 228.6 billion won in the fourth quarter of 2024, from an attributable net income of 189.8 billion won a year earlier.

Sales, however, jumped 42.6% year over year to 3.443 trillion won from 2.415 trillion won, according to a Wednesday Korea Exchange filing.

Shares of Korea Zinc fell nearly 1% in recent trade on Thursday.

data.title: Iceland Trade Deficit Narrows Sharply
data.description:
Iceland’s trade deficit narrowed sharply to ISK 7.6 billion in January 2025 from ISK 19.7 billion in the corresponding month of the previous year.

Exports soared 19% year-on-year to ISK 93.9 billion, primarily attributed to higher sales of manufacturing products (35%), farmed fish (22%), and agricultural products (12%).

Meanwhile, imports rose at a much softer pace of 3% to ISK 101.5 billion, supported by increased purchases of industrial supplies n.e.s (27%) and transport equipment (14%).

However, purchases declined for fuels and lubricants (-25%), consumer goods n.e.s (-10%), and food and beverages (-9%).

data.title: Nestcon Gets More Time to Complete Private Placement of Shares
data.description:
Nestcon secured an extension of time up to July 30 to complete the private placement, according to a Wednesday Malaysian bourse filing.

According to earlier filings, the property developer had proposed to issue up to 10% of its total issued shares to private investors.

data.title: Ayala Land Buys Back Over 2 Million Shares
data.description:
Ayala Land repurchased a further 2.1 million shares on Wednesday, according to a Thursday filing with the Philippine Stock Exchange.

The shares were repurchased between 25 Philippine pesos and 25.9 pesos apiece, the property developer said.

The company has so far repurchased around 668.3 million shares under its current buyback program.

data.title: Bulgaria Retail Sales Growth Accelerates to 8-Month High
data.description:
Retail sales in Bulgaria climbed by 7.8% year-on-year in December 2024, following a 6.8% rise in the previous month.

This marked the strongest growth in retail activity since April, boosted by higher sales of food, beverages & tobacco (9.4% vs 11% in November) and non-food products (7.3% vs 5.8%).

Meanwhile, sales of automotive fuel in specialized stores declined further (-10.7% vs -7.9%).

On a seasonally adjusted monthly basis, retail sales rose by 0.5% in December, slowing from a revised 1.4% gain in November.

data.title: CTP Enters Three New Leases with Logistics Group Milšped in Serbia
data.description:
Dutch real estate developer CTP signed three new lease agreements with Serbian logistics company Milšped for a total of 64,000 square meters, expanding their existing partnership.

Milšped will take additional space at CTParks in Belgrade and Novi Sad, Serbia, as well as at a newly developed park in Niš. CTP has so far leased a total of 105,000 square meters to Milšped, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: QualityStar Receives 2024 Global Recognition Award for Outstanding Merit in Health & Wellness Supplements
data.description:
NEW YORK, N.Y., Feb. 06, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — QualityStar, a leading innovator of multi-benefit health supplements, has received a 2024 Global Recognition Award for its transformative contributions to health and nutrition science. The accolade recognizes the company’s groundbreaking work in advancing bioavailability, nutrient synergy, and scientific research, setting new benchmarks in the industry.

The award highlights QualityStar’s commitment to bridging the gap between traditional nutrition science and innovative approaches that prioritize health outcomes. By integrating advanced research methodologies and collaborating with global institutions, QualityStar has elevated industry standards and expanded access to effective nutritional solutions.

“This award is a testament to our dedication to advancing health through rigorous science and innovation,” said QualityStar’s CEO. “Our mission is to empower individuals with evidence-based tools that support optimal wellness while upholding the highest ethical and environmental standards.”

Pioneering Advances in Health and Nutrition

QualityStar’s patented technologies have transformed how nutrients are combined and absorbed, leveraging state-of-the-art research to maximize health benefits. These innovations reflect the company’s strategic partnerships with leading global institutions, driving progress in the broader health and nutrition sector.

The company operates in FDA-compliant, GMP-certified facilities in the United States, ensuring adherence to strict quality and safety protocols. Additionally, its commitment to sustainable practices has positioned QualityStar as a leader in ethical production and environmental stewardship.

“Our success stems from a relentless focus on quality, innovation, and a deep understanding of the needs of consumers worldwide,” the CEO added.

A Global Vision for Health and Wellness

Recognized by healthcare professionals across multiple continents, QualityStar continues to champion excellence in health-focused research and development. Its work has enhanced the accessibility of nutrition science, fostering global conversations about the importance of bioavailability, nutrient synergy, and sustainable practices in health innovation.

About QualityStar

Founded by a team of renowned physicians and researchers, QualityStar has become a global leader in advancing health and nutrition. With a robust network of international collaborations and a foundation of ethical production practices, the company remains committed to fostering better health outcomes for individuals worldwide.

Contact Information:

Company Name: QualityStar

Contact Name: Emily Maxwel

Website: www.qualitystarchoice.com

Email Address: hello@qualitystarchoice.com

Location: United States

A photo accompanying this announcement is available at https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/caf4fe5a-0dce-4e8b-b4b8-f216e94546d3

data.title: News Highlights: Top Energy News of the Day - Thursday at 4 AM ET
data.description:
Orsted Expects Earnings to Rise This Year

The renewable-energy company guided for slightly higher earnings this year as some wind and solar farms ramp up, while it works to shore up its balance sheet by cutting its investment program to 2030 by 25%.

Oil prices drop on rise in U.S. supplies as tariffs threaten to dent Chinese demand

Oil futures on Wednesday marked their lowest settlement prices of the year, after official U.S. data showed a weekly climb of nearly 9 million barrels in U.S. crude inventories and as the U.S.-China trade battle threatened to dent demand.

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Rise More Than Expected

U.S. crude oil inventories increased more than expected as production picked up, while the build in gasoline stocks extended to almost three months.

Orsted Cuts 2030 Investment Program by 25%

The Danish group energy group said that 2024 Ebitda was in line with expectations, but acknowledged it experienced challenges, especially related to its U.S. offshore wind business.

Venezuela's Citgo Is Proving a Tough Sell for Bidders

The coming auction for Venezuela's Citgo Petroleum could leave its buyer on the hook to other creditors of the South American country, a stumbling block for the court-ordered sale.

Energy Is More Exposed Than Ever to a Trade War

U.S. energy is a much bigger industry than it was in President Donald Trump's first term.

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Read about U.S. oil inventories, Equinor, TotalEnergies and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

OMV's Net Profit Rises Despite Weak Refining Margins

The oil-and-gas company's refining margin fell to $5.9 a barrel, compared with $9.9 a barrel a year ago.

Oil Rises as Trump's Tariffs Raise Supply Fears

Prices jumped on concerns that U.S. tariffs against its major trade partners could disrupt supplies and raise prices at the pump.

Exxon Enters the Electricity Business, Takes a Swipe at Nuclear

Exxon says it can compete with nuclear, because the gas power plants it intends to build would capture carbon emissions.

data.title: Carnegie Access: Bergman & Beving: Interview - 20 consecutive quarters with growing EBITA
data.description:
Equity analyst Markus Almerud interviews Bergman & Beving's CEO Magnus Söderlind after the release of the company's Q3. They discuss demand, whether the slowdown in orders towards the end of the quarter has continued, what was behind the continuous margin expansion Y/Y and why it declined sequentially, the state of the M&A market and what the phase-out of unprofitable products could mean over time.

Find the interview on Carnegie Play: https://youtu.be/9pp6Kx_0HDs

This is commissioned research from Carnegie Investment Bank. Read more here: https://www.carnegie.se/cr/

data.title: Cosco Capital Repurchases More Shares
data.description:
Cosco Capital bought back 140,000 shares at 5.30 Philippine pesos apiece, according to a Thursday Philippine bourse disclosure.

The retail holding company has so far spent roughly 2.04 billion pesos out of the 4 billion pesos allocated under its buyback program.

data.title: Oil Edges Higher After Saudi Arabia Hikes Selling Prices — Market Talk
data.description: Crude futures find some support in early trade after Saudi Arabia lifted prices for March loadings of all crude grades it exports amid surging premiums for Middle Eastern oil. State-owned oil giant Aramco's flagship Arab Light crude to Asia, its main market, was raised by $2.40 a barrel to $3.90 a barrel over the Oman/Dubai benchmark--the largest monthly increase since August 2022. "This ties in with the strength that we have seen in the Middle East physical market since the start of the year," ING analysts say. Brent crude is up 0.1% at $74.71 a barrel, while WTI rises 0.3% to $71.22 a barrel. The benchmarks settled 2% lower in the previous trading session due to a larger-than-expected build in U.S. crude stockpiles and fears that a trade war between the U.S. and China could hurt global growth. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: Malaysian Shares Rise 0.7% as Trade War Concerns Ease — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index ended 0.7% higher at 1585.17. Market sentiment stayed positive as concerns of a full-scale trade war between the U.S. and China eased, alongside hopes for potential concession deals to delay or reduce tariffs, similarly to Mexico and Canada, TA Securities analyst Stephen Soo says in a note. Soo pegs the KLCI's support at 1550 and resistance at 1605. Among gainers, Petronas Gas was 2.5% higher and Gamuda Bhd. added 2.1%. Meanwhile, IOI Corp. was 1.1% lower and Mr. D.I.Y. Group lost 0.6%. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0908 GMT - Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index ended 0.7% higher at 1585.17. Market sentiment stayed positive as concerns of a full-scale trade war between the U.S. and China eased, alongside hopes for potential concession deals to delay or reduce tariffs, similarly to Mexico and Canada, TA Securities analyst Stephen Soo says in a note. Soo pegs the KLCI's support at 1550 and resistance at 1605. Among gainers, Petronas Gas was 2.5% higher and Gamuda Bhd. added 2.1%. Meanwhile, IOI Corp. was 1.1% lower and Mr. D.I.Y. Group lost 0.6%. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0905 GMT - Crude futures find some support in early trade after Saudi Arabia lifted prices for March loadings of all crude grades it exports amid surging premiums for Middle Eastern oil. State-owned oil giant Aramco's flagship Arab Light crude to Asia, its main market, was raised by $2.40 a barrel to $3.90 a barrel over the Oman/Dubai benchmark--the largest monthly increase since August 2022. "This ties in with the strength that we have seen in the Middle East physical market since the start of the year," ING analysts say. Brent crude is up 0.1% at $74.71 a barrel, while WTI rises 0.3% to $71.22 a barrel. The benchmarks settled 2% lower in the previous trading session due to a larger-than-expected build in U.S. crude stockpiles and fears that a trade war between the U.S. and China could hurt global growth. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0906 GMT - Anglo American reported a decent fourth-quarter production update, Barclays analysts write. Its key iron ore, copper and platinum group metals divisions were broadly ahead of consensus. However, sales volumes were mixed while realized prices were weaker than expected, they write. Anglo's medium-term copper guidance is unchanged but new 2027 guidance of 760,000 to 820,000 tons is lower than expected, they write. Shares trade up 4.4% at 2435.50 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0904 GMT - Orsted's 2025 Ebitda guidance of 25 billion to 28 billion Danish kroner is in line with consensus at 27.1 billion kroner, RBC Capital Markets analysts Alexander Wheeler and Ziyad Jasimuddin write. Gross investment guidance of 50 billion to 54 billion is also in line with the 52.5 billion expected by RBC. The company yesterday announced it was dropping its 2030 EBITDA and installed capacity targets, while reducing its gross investments to 2030 by around 25%. "We think that the decision to reduce the investment targets will be more of a focus on the earnings call as the market looks to Orsted's balance sheet and what impact this may have." Shares rise 5.5% to 296.30 kroner. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0848 GMT - Warpaint London's pretax profit of 24 million pounds being in line with consensus suggests margins have continued to improve from the 23% reported in 2023, RBC Capital Markets analysts Tania Maciver and Ben Pfannes-Varrow say in a note. The cosmetics company's strong performance into January, with year-on-year revenue up 15%, and continued margin improvement further endorses its strategy, the analysts say. They forecast 2025 revenue of 132 million pounds, representing on-year growth of 24%. "While the top line appears to have been impacted by continuing consumer spending headwinds, Warpaint's profit before tax... is in line with consensus and our forecasts," the analysts say. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0843 GMT - Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index closed 1.4% higher at 20891.62, tracking U.S. futures. With some easing of anxiety about U.S. tariffs, market attention is redirected to U.S. economic data releases this week, which will provide more clues about the health of the economy and the Fed's next move, ANZ Research writes in a note. BYD jumped 11.5% after the company said it will host a press conference next week about its smart-car strategy. Sunny Optical Technology gained 9.9% and SMIC was 7.2% higher. Among decliners, WH Group lost 1.6%, Baidu fell 1.3% and Galaxy Entertainment was 1.0% lower. The Hang Seng Tech Index rose 2.6% to end at 5059.38, a 2025 high. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0839 GMT - Compass Group has a high-quality earnings stream and growth prospects despite an uncertain environment, Shore Capital analyst Greg Johnson writes in a note. The London-listed catering contractor's first-quarter organic sales growth was slightly above forecast and ahead of full-year assumptions, Johnson says. Pricing and volumes will likely ease through the year, with like-for-like volumes a potential positive driver to forecasts, he adds. Shares are down 1.9% at 2,737 pence.(najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0837 GMT - WeChat's e-commerce system could gain stronger awareness among consumers in 2025 through a broader adoption of its gifting function, Citi analysts say in a research note. The Tencent-owned popular messaging app in December rolled out a gifting function, which has successfully expanded the consumption scenarios within the app's ecosystem during the Lunar New Year, the analysts note. Citi predicts the function to be more widely used, thanks to the app's "social graph" alongside a growing number of participating merchants. Citi maintains a buy rating on Tencent, with its target price unchanged at HK$563.00. Shares of Tencent last ended at HK$420.40. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0836 GMT - Carlsberg's 2024 performance and targets for 2025 are welcomed considering the challenging backdrop in the beverage sector, RBC Capital Markets analysts James Edwardes Jones and Wassachon Udomsilpa say in a note. Despite the ongoing weak consumer sentiment and the supply-chain issues, the Danish brewer's performance in Western Europe was slightly better than expected, they say. Performance in China declined 4% as it continued to be affected by a weak consumer backdrop. However, this is consistent with sector reports, the analysts add. For 2025, Carlsberg expects a relatively stable consumer environment despite ongoing uncertainty in both Asia and Europe, they say. Shares are up 3.1% at 758.60 Danish kronor. (michael.susin@wsj.com)

0824 GMT - Gucci's potential turnaround is likely to be pushed further into 2026, after parent company Kering decided to part ways with the brand's creative director Sabato De Sarno, RBC Capital Markets analysts Piral Dadhania and Richard Chamberlain write in a research note. The French luxury giant said it would announce a new artistic direction for its flagship label in due time. The termination of the collaboration doesn't come as a surprise, given Gucci's underperformance compared with peers since De Sarno's appointment, they say. A new creative direction could be the necessary next step for Gucci to recover some of the lost market share, the analysts add. Shares are down 2.7% and have dropped around 39% in the past 12 months. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0829 GMT - Pernod Ricard's guidance cut means the company is adopting a more realistic framework for its outlook that could pave the way for a share recovery down the line, Jefferies's Edward Mundy and Andrei Andon-Ionita say. The maker of Absolut vodka and Chivas Scotch whisky now forecasts a fall in organic sales for fiscal 2025, against its previous expectations of a return to growth. Consensus expectations had already moved below the company's guidance and shares in Pernod Ricard had been weak on concerns about its performance in China, the analysts say in a research note. The guidance cut should reduce the risk for Pernod Ricard's full-year earnings disappointing, Jefferies says. Shares rise 2.4%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0826 GMT - Watches of Switzerland Group's maintained guidance will likely be reassuring, Shore Capital's David Hughes writes. The London-based luxury watch retailer's third-quarter update shows encouraging signs for sales, Hughes says in a note. These include the positive performance of its businesses, improving market conditions in U.K. and continued momentum in the U.S. "Given the worsening views on the economic outlook of the U.K., and a number of recent profit downgrades from retailers following a softer seasonal period, a report of good trading and maintained fiscal 2025 guidance should reassure," he adds. Shares are down 1.8% at 544.50 pence. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

data.title: Hong Kong Business Expansion Sustained in January: PMI Report
data.description:
Hong Kong's private-sector sustained a modest expansion in January although demand from mainland China waned, reported S&P Global on Wednesday.

The headline seasonally adjusted Hong Kong purchasing managers index (PMI) logged at 51.0 in January, down marginally from 51.1 in December but still above the 50-marker that separates growth from contraction, reported S&P Global, citing its monthly survey.

Unlike for most regions or nations, for which separate PMI surveys are produced for manufacturing and service sectors, for the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region only a single PMI report is published by S&P Global, including all private-sector enterprise.

In January, Hong Kong's wholesale and retail sector was the best-performing segment, "while sharp declines in manufacturing production and construction output acted as a brake on overall growth," advised S&P Global.

Although Hong Kong business scene expanded in January, the outlook for business with the next-door behemoth mainland China was tempered. "New business from mainland China decreased to the greatest extent since April 2022. Export sales meanwhile fell for the third month running and at the fastest pace since September 2024," said S&P Global.

Hong Kong business managers in January noted sluggish global demand for goods and services. "Weak external demand was the main growth constraint in January' said S&P Global. "Export sales decreased at the fastest pace since September 2024."

Concerns about the impact of potential US tariffs on international trade were mentioned by survey respondents.

Hong Kong business managers were downbeat regarding 2025. "Hong Kong SAR firms remain pessimistic about the

year ahead business outlook," said S&P Global "Subdued business activity expectations were linked to a range of headwinds, including competitive pressures, squeezed margins, and heightened global economic uncertainty."

The Hong Kong PMI was compiled by S&P Global from surveys sent to 400 private sector companies from January 9 through January 28.

data.title: Amazon Earnings, Jobs Report, Bank of England: What to Watch Today and Friday — WSJ
data.description:
By WSJ Staff

The rest of this week brings more blue-chip earnings, plus one of the most closely watched economic data releases, the monthly jobs report. Here's a rundown:

Today

  • Earnings: Results are due premarket from drug makers including Eli Lilly, chocolate maker Hershey and videogame company Roblox. Those due to report after the close include Amazon.
  • Bank of England: Britain's central bank is expected to cut its benchmark interest rate, in a policy decision set for 7 a.m. ET. It will also give updated inflation and growth forecasts.

Tomorrow

  • Data: The nonfarm payrolls report for January is due at 8:30 a.m. Economists expect the U.S. economy added 169,000 jobs, according to a Wall Street Journal survey, down from 256,000 in December.
  • The University of Michigan's consumer-sentiment index will give a further sense of how the economy is faring at 10 a.m.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Bravida Unit Bags DKK94 Million Infrastructure Upkeep Contract in Denmark
data.description:
Swedish technical installation and services supplier Bravida said Thursday its Danish unit secured a contract worth 94 million Danish kroner with Energinet.

The eight-year deal, which will last until 2033, covers the upkeep and inspection of eight offshore facilities of the Danish national transmission system operator for electricity and natural gas.

data.title: Kone to Supply Elevators for New Life Science Campus in London
data.description:
Kone secured a contract to supply elevators to the Tribeca life science campus project in London.

The Finnish elevator and escalator company will deliver 27 Kone MonoSpace 700 DX elevators and one Kone MonoSpace 500 DX high-performance elevator, according to a Thursday release. The handover is scheduled for the third quarter of 2026.

data.title: Kao's Attributable Net Income Surges 146% in 2024
data.description:
Kao's net income attributable to owners of the parent surged 145.7% to 107.8 billion yen in the year ended Dec. 31, 2024, from 43.9 billion yen a year ago driven by better performance across its consumer product and chemical businesses.

The company's basic earnings per share (EPS) increased to 231.94 yen from 94.37 yen a year ago, according to a Tokyo Stock Exchange filing on Thursday.

Net sales rose 6.3% to 1.628 trillion yen from 1.533 trillion yen a year earlier.

It forecasted attributable net income to grow 7.6% to 116 billion yen or 249.74 yen per share, and net sales to increase 2.6% to 1.67 trillion yen for the year ending Dec. 31.

The company expects to pay interim and year-end dividends of 77.00 yen per share, each, for the current fiscal year.

data.title: Chinese Lunar New Year Retail Data Not Enough to Support Positive Outlook — Market Talk
data.description: China's retail data during Lunar New Year holiday is not enough to support a positive outlook, HSBC analysts Lina Yan and Yimin Wang in a research note. Retail sales grew 5% but the analysts don't read too much into the that as it could be merely a release of spending power after a weak 2024. Spending patterns matter more and they flag some trends that could persist in 2025. These include cost-conscious spending, more demand for certain products due to government stimulus, young consumers leading consumption. Rather than hoping for a turnaround in the consumption cycle, their top picks for 2025 include Giant Biogene for product strength; Anta for resilient earnings growth; Yum China for shareholder returns; and CR Beverage for value relative to profit growth. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Carlsberg's Slower Growth Target Is in Line With Expectations — Market Talk
data.description: Carlsberg reported a solid performance in 2024, with sales and EBIT broadly in line with expectations and delivering earnings ahead of market estimates, Citi analysts say. The Danish brewer posted surprising adjusted EPS for the year of 54.9 Danish kronor, around 5% ahead of consensus, they say in a note. Looking ahead, Carlsberg expects 2025 organic EBIT growth between 1% to 5% after including the 200bps to 300bps impact from the loss of San Miguel. "Overall, this is in line with our expectations and consensus estimates of around 3% but is slightly better than some were fearing ahead of the print," they say. EPS estimates in 2025 could be upgraded by a low single-digit percentage growth, the analysts add. Shares are up 2.1% at 858 Danish kronor. (michael.susin@wsj.com)

data.title: Italian Stocks Climb to 2008 Highs
data.description:
The FTSE MIB rose to around 36,828 in early trading on Thursday, reaching its highest level since January 2008, and recouping losses from the previous session.

The index mirrored gains across regional peers as easing concerns over escalating trade tensions lifted market sentiment.

Traders also continued to focus on upcoming earnings reports and assess the interest rate outlook.

On the domestic data front, a fresh PMI survey revealed that the Italian sector saw a second consecutive month of growth, although at a slower pace.

Among individual stocks, top performers included Ferrari (+1.2%), Unicredit (+1.2%), Tenaris (+1.4%), Poste Italiane (+1.1%), and Prysmian (+1.1%).

data.title: UBS Upgrades Wesfarmers to Neutral from Sell; Price Target is AU$76
data.description: Wesfarmers has an average rating of underperform and price targets ranging from AU$44.50 to AU$78.70, according to analysts polled by Capital IQ.

data.title: French Construction Sector Contraction Eases
data.description:
The HCOB Construction PMI in France came in at 44.5 in January 2025, up from December's 42.6, indicating the slowest rate of decline in French construction activity since November 2023.

Meanwhile, constructors were challenged by persistent demand weakness as new order inflows continued to fall sharply.

Looking ahead, businesses remained downbeat towards the outlook for activity over the coming 12 months.

data.title: State Bank of India beats profit estimates on loan, net interest income boost
data.description:
State Bank of India , the country's largest lender by assets, reported better-than-expected quarterly profit on Thursday, driven by loan growth and higher net interest income (NII).

The state-run lender reported a net profit of 168.91 billion rupees ($1.93 billion) in the third quarter, compared with 91.64 billion rupees a year ago.

Analysts, on average, were expecting a profit of 164.72 billion rupees, according to data compiled by LSEG.

SBI had registered a one-time hit of 71 billion rupees to its profit a year ago, as it set aside more provisions for wage revisions and pension costs.

The bank's employee expenses dropped 17% to 160.74 billion rupees.

The bank's NII, or the difference between interest earned on loans and paid on deposits, increased 4% to 414.46 billion rupees, as per Reuters calculations.

SBI's domestic loans grew 14.06% year-on-year.

Its gross non-performing assets ratio improved to 2.07% from 2.13% in the prior quarter.

($1 = 87.5725 Indian rupees)

data.title: Singapore fuel oil stockpiles at two-week highs
data.description:
Singapore onshore fuel oil stockpiles logged their highest volumes in two weeks, although levels dipped below the average, official data showed on Thursday.

Residue inventories (STKRS-SIN) were at 19.25 million barrels (3.03 million tons) in the week to Feb. 5, up 6.1% week-on-week, Enterprise Singapore data showed.

Volumes, however, fell below a typical average of 19.7 million barrels per week, after holding steadily higher between late December and mid-January.

Analysts eyed lower incoming supplies to Asia for February compared to last month, led by softer Russian inflows.

"Russian residuals arrivals for the first week of the month are weaker across all consuming countries," LSEG Oil Research said in a report this week.

LSEG expects arrivals to ease due to enhanced sanctions and greater scrutiny of the shadow fleet, it said.

Most of the imports flowing into Singapore onshore storage in the week to Feb. 5 were from the United States and Indonesia, based on Enterprise data, while no direct imports from Russia were recorded.

Meanwhile, most of the fuel oil exports were headed to South Korea and China for the week.

Singapore cash differentials for high-sulphur and low-sulphur fuel oil have eased slightly in recent sessions, although they remained firmer than earlier in the year.

Particularly, the high-sulphur market has been strong, though trade sources expect some cooling off in the near term.

data.title: Anglo American rises after marginally better Q4
data.description:
** Global miner Anglo American's shares up 4.6% at 2,440p; stock among top gainers on London's FTSE 100 index CURRENCYCOM:UK100, which is up 0.91%

** Posts Q4 copper production at 198 Kt, up 9% Q/Q

** Sees 2025 copper production at 690 Kt-750 kt and 760 kt-820 kt in 2026 vs 2024 production of 773 kt

** Co reduces its output forecast for rough diamonds for 2025 and 2026 on lower demand

** Analysts at Barclays call AAL's Q4 production as a decent finish to the year with Q4 production at key divisions in line with their estimates

** Peers Antofagasta up 3% and Glencore up 1.8%

** London-listed shares of Rio Tinto up 1.8%

** As of last close, AAL up ~2.4% YTD
